title: Second Saturday Explorer Ride
description:
Here’s a link to the April second Saturday ride: https://ridewithgps.com/routes/54531276

The plan is to start from Sutter High School at 9:30 a.m., bike 39 miles around the Sutter Buttes, and then, if you have time, have a late lunch in the town of Sutter at Sutter Cut Pizzeria, or maybe French Whisk or Taco Time. Because food is only available at the end of the ride, you should carry snacks.

More details below. Please let me know if you need additional information.

Thanks,

-Anne

(916) 206-2922

  • Date: Second Saturday – April 11, 2026
  • Start time: 9:30 a.m.
  • Start location: Town of Sutter: parking lot of the Sutter Union High School at 2665 Acacia Ave, Sutter, CA 95982.
  • Distance: 39 miles, ~500 feet of climbing
  • Contact: Anne Huber; anneh@dcn.org; 916-206-2922
  • Services (Food, water, bathrooms): No food until end of ride, so bring a snack. Also, no real bathrooms along the route.
  • No need to RSVP
  • Rain cancellation or postponement: communicated via the listserv.
  • Pace: 14 - 17 mph with regroups. The speed is meant to be an indicator of speed on flat terrain with no wind. People can go faster if they want as long as they are willing to regroup. There might be some stopping for scenery.
  • Route: https://ridewithgps.com/routes/54531276

 

  • For general information about the second Saturday ride and bicycling with the Davis Bike Club, please see: https://davisbikeclub.org/2nd-sat-explorer-ride/

This link includes the following: “Cycling has inherent risks. Any cyclist taking part in a DBC ride releases DBC from any liability. Guests are welcome to ride with DBC for one ride; for subsequent rides, you must be a member of the Club. By riding with a DBC group ride you acknowledge that you have read and understand this. Helmets are required on all rides.”

title: Guided Natural Wine Tasting
location: Great Bear Vineyards, Great Bear Vineyards 24800 Co Rd 101A, Davis, CA 95616
description:
Black Bicycle Farm Natural Wine Tasting in DavisSaturday, April 11 | 12:00–5:00 pm
Ready to taste wine with a little wild streak?
Step into the expressive, anything-but-ordinary world of Black Bicycle Farm at Great Bear Vineyards. This guided natural wine tasting is a deep dive into wines made from organically grown grapes, crafted with minimal intervention and maximum personality—where the vineyard, vintage, and nature itself take center stage.
 What to Expect:• A guided tasting of five distinct natural wines, each with its own story• A chance to explore orange wine, unfiltered whites, and playful carbonic reds• Insight into the philosophy, creativity, and artistry behind natural winemaking
 Featured Wines:• Squiggle 2023 – Bold, structured orange wine layered with citrus and textured depth• Orange Tipple 2023 – Perfumed with elderflower, vibrant apricot, honey, and orange notes• Clash of the Titans 2022 – Cabernet Sauvignon–Zinfandel blend with harmonious red and dark fruit• After the Fire 2020 – Cabernet Sauvignon harvested during the 2020 wildfires; smoky, spicy, and unforgettable• Carbo Cab 2019 – Playful, unfiltered Cabernet bursting with Bing cherry and candied strawberry, lifted by carbonic maceration
Set against the rustic elegance of our winery, this tasting celebrates innovation, sustainability, and letting nature do the talking—guided by people who are genuinely passionate about the craft.
Whether you’re a longtime natural wine fan or simply curious to try something new, this is a tasting that promises to be thought-provoking, delicious, and full of character.
 Tickets required. Come sip outside the box with us!

title: Mosaics Intercultural Dinner Fellowship
description:
Davis Art, Music & Outdoor Activities Meetup
**Discover Intercultural Mosaics This Winter 2025–2026!**

Join us for a vibrant and uplifting season of virtual learning and community from **December 20, 2025 to March 28, 2026**—all from the comfort of your home on Zoom!

**Registration is required, and the Zoom link will be provided exclusively to those who sign up.**

**Bi-Weekly Friday Zoom Sessions** *(Every 2nd and 4th Friday)*
• **4:00 PM** – Advanced Korean with Stephen
• **5:00 PM** – Intermediate Conversational Chinese with Sam
• **6:00 PM** – Bible Study & Meditation Group with Stephen
• **7:00 PM** – Beginning Chinese with Jane

**Weekly Saturday Zoom Sessions** (total of 12 sessions)
• **4:00 PM** – Beginning Spanish with Viviana
• **5:00 PM** – Beginning to Intermediate Korean with Stephen
• **6:00 PM** – Beginning to Intermediate Japanese with Norio
• **7:00 PM** – English Learners Group with Linda & Chad

**Important Notes:**

1. **No Zoom sessions** on **January 2–3, 2026** (New Year) and **April 3–4, 2026** (Easter).
2. **Special Gatherings (Tentative):**
**• February 14, 2026:** Valentine’s Day & pre–Lunar New Year celebration in person at Phoenix Hall, 2nd Floor, Davis Community Church.
**• March 14, 2026:** Additional in-person gathering (tentative).

**Return to In-Person Meetings:**
We will resume our **regular in-person gatherings on April 11, 2026** at the **2nd floor rooms of Phoenix Hall, Davis Community Church**.

For more information about Intercultural Mosaics classes and activities, visit **[www.nextg.org](http://www.nextg.org)** or contact **Dr. Stephen Moon** at **916-217-5470** or **catalyst@nextg.org**.

title: Raíces en Movimiento
location: International House Davis, 10 College Park, Davis, CA, 95616, United States
description:
A dynamic gathering that weaves together dance, history, and musical exploration to celebrate stories of Latin American migration. 
Part of the I-House World Tour: Roots & Routes series, Raíces en Movimiento is a multidisciplinary performance that weaves together dance, storytelling, poetry, and historical reflection to honor the sacrifices and resilience of Latin American migration. 
Through movement and voice, the performance explores themes of displacement, identity, and the search for belonging, while celebrating the ways culture continues to connect communities across borders and generations. Traditional and contemporary dances from across Latin America are paired with original narration and poetry, transforming migration stories into a shared rhythm of resilience, memory, and joy. 
More than a performance, this gathering invites community members to pause, reflect, and celebrate the power of heritage and movement as pathways for healing, connection, and cultural memory. Following the performance, guests are invited to stay, connect, and continue the conversation in community.

title: Pick Your Own Bouquet at Park Winters
location: Park Winters, Park Winters, 27850 County Road 26 Winters, CA 95694
description:
This seasonal farm hospitality experience, offered most Saturdays and Sundays, includes everything you need to create a beautiful farm-grown bouquet. Wander through the flower rows and enjoy a peaceful countryside outing while our team guides you on how to clip and gather your blooms. 
Your freshly picked flowers will be wrapped in a moisture-soaked paper wrap to keep them fresh until they are placed in a vase at home. 
While we will accommodate walk-in guests when availability allows, reservations are recommended. On the booking page, simply select the number of bouquets you would like to create. 
As this experience is guided by nature, flower varieties change throughout the season. If field conditions do not meet expectations, a full refund is available upon request.

title: Natural Wine Tasting
location: Great Bear Vineyards, Great Bear Vineyards 24800 Co Rd 101A, Davis, CA 95616
description:
Natural Wine Tasting at Great Bear Vineyards in Davis 
Sunday, April 12 | 12–3pm
Curious about natural wine? Come explore something beautifully different. Step into the world of Black Bicycle Farm’s natural, orange, and uniquely expressive wines—where minimal intervention lets nature take the lead and every glass tells a story. 
This guided tasting is a journey through purity, creativity, and vineyard character, perfect for both seasoned wine lovers and the naturally curious.
 What to Expect:
A guided tasting of five distinct natural wines
Explore orange wines, unfiltered whites, and vibrant reds
Learn about carbonic maceration and low-intervention winemaking
Discover the philosophy behind letting nature shine in every bottle
 Featured Wines Include:
Squiggle 2023 — bold, textured orange wine with bright citrus layers
Orange Tipple 2023 — floral and aromatic with apricot, honey & orange notes
Clash of the Titans 2022 — a harmonious Cabernet Sauvignon–Zinfandel blend
After the Fire 2020 — smoky, spicy Cabernet with a powerful story
Carbo Cab 2019 — playful, unfiltered Cabernet bursting with cherry and strawberry
Set against the rustic elegance of the vineyard, this is your chance to sip, learn, and experience wines that are as thoughtful as they are delicious.
 Come discover something new, sip something unexpected, and enjoy a Sunday afternoon full of character at Great Bear Vineyards.

title: Conscious Conversations & Embodied Listening
description:
Iama Embodied Community
This is a spiritual community that honors the body’s wisdom and lived experience. Whether you’re healing from religious trauma, deconstructing beliefs, exploring spirituality, or just want to be with other conscious persons, this space is for you.

Expect conscious conversations, meditation, breathwork, sound healing, and other somatic practices to help reconnect with ourselves and each other.

we share a potluck dinner every last Monday of the month.

📅 **Date & Time**: Mondays 6-8 PM
📍 **Location**: 727 T St. Sacramento, Ca, 95811
💜 **All our events are free, donations supported.**
✨ **No prior experience or spiritual beliefs necessary.**
📩 **RSVP/ Questions? Reach out at** embody.iama@gmail.com
🔗 **Follow us on Instagram:** [@embody.iama](https://www.instagram.com/embody.iama)

PS: we meet in the social hall of Parkview Presbyterian Church. Please find us through the back entrance, the brown gate on Solons alley. You may park in the back, move the orange post to park and please return it when you leave.

title: Weekly Game Night at Blue Note
description:
Yolo County Board Game Gathering
Update 8/14/25:
**We're still active**, but we primarily use Discord to chat and plan events. (See group description for invite link.)

We still meet every Wednesday for board games at Blue Note Brewing Company. We'll have a handful of tables reserved near the back; just look for the pile of board games.

People usually start arriving around 5pm, and games start whenever people are ready. Blue Note closes at 9pm, and although we have a 5-10 minute grace period there, you should be in the process of final scoring by 9, or else pack it up and play again another day.

Game night is open to all ages! Blue Note is a brewery, not a bar, so there's no restriction against minors. In addition to their crafted-in-house beer selection, water and sodas are available.

You're not required to buy anything in order to join us, but we do encourage you to support local businesses. Blue Note's chef team makes a mean pizza, and there are other nearby downtown restaurants and businesses.
source: Meetup Yolo County Board Game Gathering

title: Free Yoga Practice
description:
Iama Embodied Community
🌀 **Move with intention. Connect with your body. Celebrate community.**

Join us every **Thursday at 6 PM** for an embodied yoga practice designed to help you release tension, ground yourself, and cultivate presence. This is a trauma-informed, all-levels class, no prior yoga experience needed. Expect a Hatha-Vinyasa flow, breathwork, and a space to listen to your body’s wisdom.

🌿 **Every last Thursday of the month, stay after class for a community potluck dinner!**

📍 **Location**: 727 T St. Sacramento CA 95811
✨ **All our events are free. Donations are welcome.**
📩 **Questions? Reach out at** embody.iama@gmail.com
🔗 **Follow us on Instagram:** [@embody.iama](https://www.instagram.com/embody.iama)

title: Live Music and Food Truck
location: Great Bear Vineyards, Great Bear Vineyards 24800 Co Rd 101A, Davis, CA 95616
description:
Live Music & Food Truck Night at Great Bear Vineyards in Davis 
Friday, April 17 | 5–8pmTicket Includes: Glass of Wine & Live Music
Join us for a special Friday evening celebrating music, community, and local talent at the vineyard! 
We’re thrilled to feature the Davis Senior High School Jazz Band, bringing smooth jazz, energy, and Blue Devil pride to the vines. Sit back, relax, and enjoy an unforgettable live performance in a beautiful, laid-back setting.
 Buenos Aires Grill will be onsite serving up their delicious empanadas and authentic Argentinian bites—perfectly paired with your included glass of wine and more available by the glass or bottle.
Sip, savor, and soak in the sounds of these talented young musicians while supporting the next generation of artists. 
 Great music. Delicious food. A chance to give back.The perfect way to spend your Friday night—we hope to see you there!

title: Davis Farmers Market
location: Central Park, 301 C Street, Davis, CA, 95616, United States
description:
Year-round at Central Park in downtown Davis, thousands gather each week to shop for what is grown, raised, and made locally at our markets. We are dedicated to bringing farmers right to your table, so we stay open rain or shine. 
Walk rows of stalls lined with an array of fruits and vegetables, including certified organic produce, choice meats, chicken, seafood, wine, local eggs and honey. Choose from a variety of fresh-baked goods, plants, flowers and gifts. Enjoy a picnic from one of our many food stands, made from market ingredients.

title: Picnic Day
location: UC Davis, One Shields Ave, Davis, 95616, United States
description:
Since its inception in 1909, Picnic Day in Davis has become the crown-jewel event of the UC system and the signature event of UC Davis. One of the largest student-run events in the nation, Picnic Day showcases the richness of diversity and achievement at UC Davis and the surrounding community in the areas of research, teaching, service, and campus life. 
This long-standing campus tradition began in 1909 when the University Farm invited the surrounding community to view their new dairy barn. Two thousand visitors attended, bringing picnics to complement the coffee, cream, and sugar provided by the University. Following the success of the 1909 picnic, the faculty of the University Farm continued to plan and sponsor the event until a student committee took over the task in 1912. Through the years of Picnic Day history, the event has only been canceled five times. In 1924, an outbreak of hoof-and-mouth disease among the cowherds caused the first cancellation. In 1938, delayed construction of the gymnasium, which was needed to accommodate the ever-increasing number of participants, led to a second cancellation. During World War II, the Army Signal Corps controlled the campus, and Picnic Day disappeared from 1943 to 1945. Since 1946, Picnic Day has been growing strong and now boasts an annual attendance of more than 70,000 people. This year, there will be more than 200 events on campus and an estimated 75,000 visitors attending this special event. Since 1959, the parade was extended to include downtown Davis to celebrate the fact that Davis became a separate UC campus and not just the Farm School for UC Berkeley. 
Click here to learn about the events happening during Picnic Day 2026.

title: Chocolate and Wine Pairing
location: Great Bear Vineyards, Great Bear Vineyards 24800 Co Rd 101A, Davis, CA 95616
description:
Wine & Chocolate Pairing Experience in Davis 
Saturday, April 18 | 12–5pm
Indulge your senses with a decadent afternoon of wine and chocolate at its finest. Join us at Great Bear Vineyards for a tasting experience that celebrates the irresistible harmony between our handcrafted wines and silky Lindt chocolates. 
Each pairing is thoughtfully curated to highlight the interplay of flavor, texture, and aroma—from bright bubbles to rich, velvety reds and luscious dessert wines. Take your time, savor each bite and sip, and discover just how magical this pairing can be.
 Suggested Pairings:
– White Chocolate (Gold Wrapper) → Sparkling Brut (Méthode Champenoise)Crisp bubbles meet creamy sweetness for an elegant finish
– Caramel Chocolate (Dark Gold Wrapper) → MalbecLayers of cherry, blackberry, spice, and caramel richness
– Pistachio Chocolate (Green Wrapper) → White Port-Style Dessert WineNutty, aromatic, and beautifully honeyed
– Dark Chocolate 70% (Black & Gold Wrapper) → Ruby Port-Style Dessert WineDeep, intense, and perfectly balanced
 Prefer to keep it sparkling? You’re welcome to enjoy two flutes of our Sparkling Brut in place of the pairings.
Unwrap, sip, and treat yourself to a little moment of indulgence in the vineyard. Because some pairings are simply meant to be.

title: UC Davis Picnic Day Farm-to-Fork Dinner
location: Great Bear Vineyards, Great Bear Vineyards 24800 Co Rd 101A, Davis, CA 95616
description:
Farm-to-Fork Dinner at Great Bear Vineyards in Davis — UC Davis Picnic Day Saturday, April 18 | 5:00–8:00 pm Just 3 miles from downtown Davis
After a full day of fun, traditions, and high-energy excitement at UC Davis Picnic Day, slip away from the crowds and wind down in the vineyard. Join us at Great Bear Vineyards for a relaxed Farm-to-Fork Dinner with live music, set among our organic vines and glowing in the golden light of sunset.
Whether you’re an Aggie alum, a current student, or a local looking to end the day on a high note, this is your invitation to sip, savor, and celebrate spring with great food, great wine, and even better company.
 Spring Farm-to-Fork Dinner MenuA chef-prepared feast highlighting fresh, locally sourced ingredients:• Mixed greens salad with seasonal local produce• Creamy mashed potatoes with sweet corn• Seasonal roasted vegetables• Grilled tri-tip with smoky BBQ sauce & creamed horseradish• Italian herb chicken topped with fresh tomato bruschetta• Mediterranean succotash with chickpeas, beans & garden vegetables• Bite-sized desserts for a sweet finish
 Wine Available for Purchase:• Glass of wine — $10• Bottles of Chardonnay, Rosé, or Red Blend — $38
Settle in with live music, a cozy vineyard atmosphere, and the perfect chance to slow things down after the bustle of Picnic Day. This is what spring evenings in Davis are all about.
 Great food. Great wine. Great vibes. Go Aggies!

title: Art in Action: Woven Fairy House Wall Hangings
description:
Pence Family Art Workshops
Our fairy houses are back – with a twist! Learn how to make a simple loom with sticks and twine, and weave flowers, leaves, and other natural materials to create your very own wall hanging. Decorate your woven art piece with moss, fabric butterflies, beads, and more to create the perfect fairy hideaway.

Art in Action: Woven Fairy House Wall Hangings
Sunday, April 19, 1-3 PM
$5 Members, $10 Non-Members (per person, all materials included)

**How it works:**
Art in Action engages kids in art projects that are fun, creative, and collaborative with their family members. This is a drop-in program for ages 5-12. No registration in advance is required, but space and supplies are limited to the first 25 participants and are available on a first come, first served basis.

When you arrive, pay with cash or card at the front desk. A parent or adult caregiver is required to stay, but only has to pay for the project for themselves if they are also doing art. Stay as long or as short as you like over the two hour period.

Become a Member starting at $70/year and save! If you join the Pence Gallery when you attend this workshop, you can enjoy Member pricing for Art in Action, plus receive a $5 gift certificate to our gift shop as a thank you. Membership supports art for all ages and makes creative moments like this possible—jump in and be part of it.

We have a different Art in Action family workshop every month! Learn more at [https://pencegallery.org/art-in-action/](https://pencegallery.org/art-in-action/).

This unique monthly program is generously sponsored and made possible by Mary Stephens DeWall and Les DeWall.

title: Sip Yolo with Yolo County Wines
location: Yolo County Vineyard and Winery Association, 13 winery locations!

title: Live Music and Food Truck
location: Great Bear Vineyards, Great Bear Vineyards 24800 Co Rd 101A, Davis, CA 95616
description:
Live Music & Food Truck Night at Great Bear Vineyards in Davis 
Friday, April 24 | 5–8pmTicket Includes: Glass of Wine & Live Music
Kick back and start your weekend the Great Bear way—with a glass of wine in hand and vineyard views all around. 
We’ve got another beautiful Friday evening lined up featuring live music from the Wealth of Nations Duo, bringing their signature laid-back, feel-good sound to the vineyard. From Grateful Dead favorites to classic rock, R&B, and original songs, their set is the perfect soundtrack for a relaxing night under the open sky. 
 Come hungry! GyroStop will be onsite serving up fresh, flavorful Mediterranean favorites—think gyro sandwiches, falafel, hummus, and more—all crafted by Chef Ty and perfect for pairing with your favorite estate-grown wines.
Sip, relax, and settle in for an easygoing evening of great music, delicious food, and even better company.
 The perfect way to unwind and welcome the weekend—we can’t wait to see you there!

title: Yolo Wine Ramble
description:
Ramble around Yolo County’s wine country & discover the wine country you didn’t know you were missing! Ramble around the county exploring all of Yolo’s wine trail and incredible wineries. 
April 25th & 26th 
With our Wine Ramble tickets, you and a friend get buy one, get one wine flight (BOGO) all weekend long! Sip, Savor, and stay the weekend with our association wineries. Our 14 participating members are family-owned, estate-grown and award-winning. Ramble weekend details are below – create your own ramble itinerary or use our suggested Yolo wine trails!

title: Davis Farmers Market
location: Central Park, 301 C Street, Davis, CA, 95616, United States
description:
Year-round at Central Park in downtown Davis, thousands gather each week to shop for what is grown, raised, and made locally at our markets. We are dedicated to bringing farmers right to your table, so we stay open rain or shine. 
Walk rows of stalls lined with an array of fruits and vegetables, including certified organic produce, choice meats, chicken, seafood, wine, local eggs and honey. Choose from a variety of fresh-baked goods, plants, flowers and gifts. Enjoy a picnic from one of our many food stands, made from market ingredients.

title: Pick Your Own Bouquet at Park Winters
location: Park Winters, Park Winters, 27850 County Road 26 Winters, CA 95694
description:
This seasonal farm hospitality experience, offered most Saturdays and Sundays, includes everything you need to create a beautiful farm-grown bouquet. Wander through the flower rows and enjoy a peaceful countryside outing while our team guides you on how to clip and gather your blooms. 
Your freshly picked flowers will be wrapped in a moisture-soaked paper wrap to keep them fresh until they are placed in a vase at home. 
While we will accommodate walk-in guests when availability allows, reservations are recommended. On the booking page, simply select the number of bouquets you would like to create. 
As this experience is guided by nature, flower varieties change throughout the season. If field conditions do not meet expectations, a full refund is available upon request.

title: Sip Yolo with Yolo County Wines
location: Yolo County Vineyard and Winery Association, 13 winery locations!

title: Park Winters Garden Tour
location: Park Winters, Park Winters, 27850 County Road 26 Winters, CA 95694
description:
Park Winters is surrounded by hundreds of acres of farmland with unobstructed views of the Berryessa Blue Ridge Reserve. Originally planted in 1865, our gardens take you back in time—now lovingly curated, manicured and maintained. 
Join us for a guided tour, take in the beauty of 150 year old trees and stroll through our sunken garden filled with lavender and snowy white roses. The classic cypress trees soar and native songbirds call as we experience the scents and scenes of the western Yolo County countryside. This experience is approximately a 90 minute walking tour, 
*Garden Tours are on the 2nd and 4th Saturday’s of each month, with exception for July – August, when we take a pause due to high temperatures – Garden Tours will resume in September.

title: Mosaics Acoustic Guitar & Singing Group
description:
Davis Art, Music & Outdoor Activities Meetup
**Discover Intercultural Mosaics This Winter 2025–2026!**

Join us for a vibrant and uplifting season of virtual learning and community from **December 20, 2025 to March 28, 2026**—all from the comfort of your home on Zoom!

**Registration is required, and the Zoom link will be provided exclusively to those who sign up.**

**Bi-Weekly Friday Zoom Sessions** *(Every 2nd and 4th Friday)*
• **4:00 PM** – Advanced Korean with Stephen
• **5:00 PM** – Intermediate Conversational Chinese with Sam
• **6:00 PM** – Bible Study & Meditation Group with Stephen
• **7:00 PM** – Beginning Chinese with Jane

**Weekly Saturday Zoom Sessions** (total of 12 sessions)
• **4:00 PM** – Beginning Spanish with Viviana
• **5:00 PM** – Beginning to Intermediate Korean with Stephen
• **6:00 PM** – Beginning to Intermediate Japanese with Norio
• **7:00 PM** – English Learners Group with Linda & Chad

**Important Notes:**

1. **No Zoom sessions** on **January 2–3, 2026** (New Year) and **April 3–4, 2026** (Easter).
2. **Special Gatherings (Tentative):**
**• February 14, 2026:** Valentine’s Day & pre–Lunar New Year celebration in person at Phoenix Hall, 2nd Floor, Davis Community Church.
**• March 14, 2026:** Additional in-person gathering (tentative).

**Return to In-Person Meetings:**
We will resume our **regular in-person gatherings on April 11, 2026** at the **2nd floor rooms of Phoenix Hall, Davis Community Church**.

For more information about Intercultural Mosaics classes and activities, visit **[www.nextg.org](http://www.nextg.org)** or contact **Dr. Stephen Moon** at **916-217-5470** or **catalyst@nextg.org**.

title: Rustic Ramble – A Capay Valley Grown Farm and Garden Trail
location: Polestar Farms, 25491 Co Rd 21A, Esparto, 95627, United States
description:
The Rustic Ramble is a self-guided farm and garden tour designed to connect visitors with the land, the farmers, and the beauty that define the Capay Valley. Wander orchards and working farms, explore private gardens rarely open to the public, meet the growers (and a few animals), and experience the valley at your own pace — one meaningful stop at a time.

Whether you’re here for the food, the farming, or a slow day in the countryside, the Rustic Ramble offers a deeper way to experience the Capay Valley. Proceeds from the event support Capay Valley Vision and Capay Valley Grown, helping sustain local agriculture, community programs, and future agritourism events in the Capay Valley.

Open Farm & Garden Trail: Invites participants to explore 20+ locations throughout the Capay Valley. Highlights include a ranch specializing in Arabian horses, private gardens brimming with California native plants, lavender farms, 40-year-old established cottage gardens, and fields producing organic fruits and vegetables and nuts.

Taste of Capay Picnic: Taste the products from al the farms you visit! At the Taste of Capay picnic enjoy small bites from local chefs, a beautiful grazing table, wine and olive oil samples and live music at Polestar Farm in Esparto!

title: Sip Yolo with Yolo County Wines
location: Yolo County Vineyard and Winery Association, 13 winery locations!

title: International Karaoke Night @ I-House
location: International House Davis, 10 College Park, Davis, CA, 95616, United States
description:
International Karaoke Night is a high energy celebration of music, culture, and community in Davis. Sing songs from around the world — from favorites from your home country to global hits and timeless classics. No karaoke experience required! Everyone is welcome! 
This evening is all about fun and connection, not performance. Come solo or with friends. Sing your heart out, cheer others on, hit the dance floor, or simply soak in the atmosphere in a welcoming, low pressure space. Between performances, there will be line dancing and raffle prizes given away! 
Discover music in different languages, meet new people, and take part in a joyful evening that brings cultures together through shared experience. Get your ticket and join us for a night where community is built one song at a time.

title: Conscious Conversations & Embodied Listening
description:
Iama Embodied Community
This is a spiritual community that honors the body’s wisdom and lived experience. Whether you’re healing from religious trauma, deconstructing beliefs, exploring spirituality, or just want to be with other conscious persons, this space is for you.

Expect conscious conversations, meditation, breathwork, sound healing, and other somatic practices to help reconnect with ourselves and each other.

title: Weekly Game Night at Blue Note
description:
Yolo County Board Game Gathering
Update 8/14/25:
**We're still active**, but we primarily use Discord to chat and plan events. (See group description for invite link.)

We still meet every Wednesday for board games at Blue Note Brewing Company. We'll have a handful of tables reserved near the back; just look for the pile of board games.

People usually start arriving around 5pm, and games start whenever people are ready. Blue Note closes at 9pm, and although we have a 5-10 minute grace period there, you should be in the process of final scoring by 9, or else pack it up and play again another day.

Game night is open to all ages! Blue Note is a brewery, not a bar, so there's no restriction against minors. In addition to their crafted-in-house beer selection, water and sodas are available.

You're not required to buy anything in order to join us, but we do encourage you to support local businesses. Blue Note's chef team makes a mean pizza, and there are other nearby downtown restaurants and businesses.
source: Meetup Yolo County Board Game Gathering

title: Floral Arranging Workshop
location: Bicycling Hall of Fame, 303 3rd St,, Davis, CA, 95616, United States
description: Floral arranging workshop with Sarah Larkin on April 30 in Davis. Grab a friend and join us for a relaxing evening of wine, light appetizers, and beautiful flowers. Sarah will share simple tips and techniques to help you create a fresh spring bouquet perfect for your table or your next gathering. Each participant will leave with their own floral arrangement and the confidence to recreate the design at home. All proceeds support children and families served by the Yolo Crisis Nursery.

title: Healing Arts 6-Session Workshop
description:
Pence Adult Art Programs
The Pence’s 6-session Healing Arts workshop uses art as a healing language for those experiencing grief from the loss of a loved one. Through the process of art making, participants will have opportunities for discussion, education, and connecting with peers in a safe and supportive space in the Pence Learning Center under the guidance of facilitators Amy Vidra and Lyndsey Rocca.

REGISTRATION ONLINE IS REQUIRED IN ADVANCE through the Pence Gallery's website, and space is limited to the first 8 participants who register. Participants must be 18 or older, and no art experience is necessary. This program is generously sponsored by Bob & Joanne Andresen.

Healing Arts 6-Session Workshop
Fridays\, 10\-11:45 AM \| May 1\, 8\, 15\, 22\, 29 & June 5
$25 Members, $35 Non-Members (all materials included)

**Reminder - an RSVP on Meetup does NOT count as registration. Registration is required in advance through the [Pence’s website (CLICK HERE)](https://pencegallery.org/education/programs/workshops-and-classes/).**

\*Please note that when registering for this program, you are signing up for all 6 sessions. If you are unable to attend one or more of the sessions, please let us know in advance.

Any questions? Email pencegallery@gmail.com.

title: Blood on the Clocktower at Blue Note
description:
Yolo County Board Game Gathering
The first Wednesday of every month, we play _Blood on the Clocktower_, a social deduction game in the vein of Werewolf/Mafia/The Resistance, with a few twists!

We'll have a circle of chairs reserved inside Blue Note, near the back, with a Yolo County Board Game Gathering sign on the table. Just say hello, and we'll show you some games, pull some players together, and get to having fun!

We also usually have a few tables of players playing other board games, if social deduction isn't your bag!

Get a feel for Blood on the Clocktower here: https://youtu.be/4sfa8_kNxsQ
source: Meetup Yolo County Board Game Gathering

title: Urban Sketching with Pete Scully
description:
Pence Adult Art Programs
Urban sketching is a fun and accessible way to see and connect with where you live with a sketchbook! In this one-day workshop with Pete Scully, students will draw in ink and pencil and watercolor, and learn some principles of perspective and composition. You’ll explore how to ‘see’ what to sketch, discover how to ‘un-puzzle’ an urban scene, and let your sketches tell a story. Students will also loosen up with some quick people sketches! Sketching will be done both inside and outside; if you like to sit when you sketch, a light portable stool is recommended. Artists of all levels are welcome, including beginners. Prior drawing experience is helpful, but not required.

Urban Sketching with Pete Scully
Saturday, May 9, 10 AM - 4 PM
$140 Members, $160 Non-Members (Materials not included)

**Registration is required through the [Pence's website (CLICK HERE)](https://pencegallery.org/education/programs/workshops-and-classes/).**

Any questions? Email pencegallery@gmail.com.

title: Artist Talk & Reception with Linda Clark Johnson
description:
Pence Adult Art Programs
Join artist Linda Clark Johnson as she discusses her artistic process in working with cyanotype, including her love of sunlight and her ways of gathering and arranging plants. Linda will also show visitors how to get started with cyanotype through an artist demo, as well as explore her current work in her exhibit, *Sunshine & Shadows*, on view at the Pence Gallery from May 2 - June 21, 2026.

Artist Talk & Reception with Linda Clark Johnson
Sunday, May 17, 1-2:30 PM
Free Admission

title: Healing Arts One-Time Workshop
description:
Pence Adult Art Programs
Part of the Pence's Healing Arts Program and run by facilitator Amy Vidra, this one-time workshop focuses more broadly on themes related to depression, anxiety, or major life changes. Through the process of art making, participants will have opportunities for discussion, education, and connecting with peers in a safe and supportive space.

REGISTRATION ONLINE IS REQUIRED IN ADVANCE through the Pence Gallery's website, and space is limited to the first 8 participants who register. Participants must be 18 or older, and no art experience is necessary. This program is generously sponsored by Bob & Joanne Andresen.

Healing Arts One-Time Workshop
Monday, May 18, 10-11:45 AM
$5 Members, $10 Non-Members (all materials included)

**Reminder - an RSVP on Meetup does NOT count as registration. Registration is required in advance through the [Pence’s website (CLICK HERE)](https://pencegallery.org/education/programs/workshops-and-classes/).**

\*If you register for this session and are unable to attend, please let us know in advance.

Any questions? Email pencegallery@gmail.com.

title: Weekly Game Night at Blue Note
description:
Yolo County Board Game Gathering
Update 8/14/25:
**We're still active**, but we primarily use Discord to chat and plan events. (See group description for invite link.)

We still meet every Wednesday for board games at Blue Note Brewing Company. We'll have a handful of tables reserved near the back; just look for the pile of board games.

People usually start arriving around 5pm, and games start whenever people are ready. Blue Note closes at 9pm, and although we have a 5-10 minute grace period there, you should be in the process of final scoring by 9, or else pack it up and play again another day.

Game night is open to all ages! Blue Note is a brewery, not a bar, so there's no restriction against minors. In addition to their crafted-in-house beer selection, water and sodas are available.

You're not required to buy anything in order to join us, but we do encourage you to support local businesses. Blue Note's chef team makes a mean pizza, and there are other nearby downtown restaurants and businesses.
source: Meetup Yolo County Board Game Gathering

title: Art in Action: Spectacular Seed Bombs
description:
Pence Family Art Workshops
Learn about the ancient Japanese practice of seed bombs! Using air-dry clay and native wildflower seeds, make seed bombs that you can later plant outdoors or in a garden. Decorate your seed bombs with dried petals and stamps, and design and illustrate a plant care card to help you take care of the wildflowers you plant.

Art in Action: Spectacular Seed Bombs
Saturday, May 30, 1-3 PM
$5 Members, $10 Non-Members (per person, all materials included)

**How it works:**
Art in Action engages kids in art projects that are fun, creative, and collaborative with their family members. This is a drop-in program for ages 5-12. No registration in advance is required, but space and supplies are limited to the first 25 participants and are available on a first come, first served basis.

When you arrive, pay with cash or card at the front desk. A parent or adult caregiver is required to stay, but only has to pay for the project for themselves if they are also doing art. Stay as long or as short as you like over the two hour period.

Become a Member starting at $70/year and save! If you join the Pence Gallery when you attend this workshop, you can enjoy Member pricing for Art in Action, plus receive a $5 gift certificate to our gift shop as a thank you. Membership supports art for all ages and makes creative moments like this possible—jump in and be part of it.

We have a different Art in Action family workshop every month! Learn more at [https://pencegallery.org/art-in-action/](https://pencegallery.org/art-in-action/).

This unique monthly program is generously sponsored and made possible by Mary Stephens DeWall and Les DeWall.

title: Blood on the Clocktower at Blue Note
description:
Yolo County Board Game Gathering
The first Wednesday of every month, we play _Blood on the Clocktower_, a social deduction game in the vein of Werewolf/Mafia/The Resistance, with a few twists!

We'll have a circle of chairs reserved inside Blue Note, near the back, with a Yolo County Board Game Gathering sign on the table. Just say hello, and we'll show you some games, pull some players together, and get to having fun!

We also usually have a few tables of players playing other board games, if social deduction isn't your bag!

Get a feel for Blood on the Clocktower here: https://youtu.be/4sfa8_kNxsQ
source: Meetup Yolo County Board Game Gathering
